2603.17150.title: Intent Formalization: A Grand Challenge for Reliable Coding in the Age of AI Agents
2603.17150.paper_summary: Agentic AI systems can now generate code with remarkable fluency, but a fundamental question remains: \emph{does the generated code actually do what the user intended?} The gap between informal natural language requirements and precise program behavior -- the \emph{intent gap} -- has always plagued software engineering, but AI-generated code amplifies it to an unprecedented scale. This article argues that \textbf{intent formalization} -- the translation of informal user intent into a set of checkable formal specifications -- is the key challenge that will determine whether AI makes software more reliable or merely more abundant. Intent formalization offers a tradeoff spectrum suitable to the reliability needs of different contexts: from lightweight tests that disambiguate likely misinterpretations, through full functional specifications for formal verification, to domain-specific languages from which correct code is synthesized automatically. The central bottleneck is \emph{validating specifications}: since there is no oracle for specification correctness other than the user, we need semi-automated metrics that can assess specification quality with or without code, through lightweight user interaction and proxy artifacts such as tests. We survey early research that demonstrates the \emph{potential} of this approach: interactive test-driven formalization that improves program correctness, AI-generated postconditions that catch real-world bugs missed by prior methods, and end-to-end verified pipelines that produce provably correct code from informal specifications. We outline the open research challenges -- scaling beyond benchmarks, achieving compositionality over changes, metrics for validating specifications, handling rich logics, designing human-AI specification interactions -- that define a research agenda spanning AI, programming languages, formal methods, and human-computer interaction.
2603.17150.paper_summary_zh: 代理式人工智慧系統現在能夠以驚人的流暢度生成代碼，但一個根本性問題仍然存在：\emph{生成的代碼實際上是否符合用戶的意圖？} 非正式自然語言需求與精確程序行為之間的差距——\emph{意圖差距}——一直困擾著軟體工程，但人工智慧生成的代碼將其放大到前所未有的規模。本文主張，\textbf{意圖形式化}——將非正式用戶意圖轉化為一組可檢查的正式規範——是決定人工智慧是否能使軟體更可靠或僅僅是更豐富的關鍵挑戰。意圖形式化提供了一個適合不同情境可靠性需求的權衡光譜：從輕量級測試以消除可能的誤解，通過完整功能規範進行正式驗證，到從中自動合成正確代碼的領域特定語言。核心瓶頸是\emph{驗證規範}：由於除了用戶之外沒有任何關於規範正確性的神諭，我們需要半自動化的度量來評估規範質量，無論是否有代碼，通過輕量級用戶互動和代理工件，如測試。我們調查了早期研究，展示了這種方法的\emph{潛力}：互動式測試驅動的形式化提高了程序正確性，人工智慧生成的後置條件捕捉到了先前方法遺漏的現實世界錯誤，以及從非正式規範產生可證明正確代碼的端到端驗證管道。我們概述了定義研究議程的開放研究挑戰——超越基準的擴展、在變更上實現組合性、驗證規範的度量、處理豐富邏輯、設計人機規範互動——這些挑戰跨越了人工智慧、程式語言、正式方法和人機互動。
2603.17150.author: Shuvendu K. Lahiri et.al.
